title: Exercises: Implementation and Performance
description: Hands-on exercises for implementing and optimizing hash-based signatures

exercises.title: Constant-Time Primitives
exercises.description: Implement constant-time operations for secure cryptographic code.
exercises.task: Create constant-time comparison and selection functions.
hints: XOR all bytes and OR results for compare
Use mask = -int(cond) for all-1s or all-0s

exercises.title: Hash Function Benchmarking
exercises.description: Benchmark different hash functions for signature use.
exercises.task: Create a benchmarking tool for hash operations.
exercises.starterCode:
import hashlib
import time
from typing import Dict

class HashBenchmark:
    def __init__(self, iterations: int = 10000):
        self.iterations = iterations
    
    def benchmark_all(self, data_size: int = 64) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Benchmark all hash functions, return times in microseconds."""
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def estimate_wots_time(self, n: int, w: int, hash_us: float) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Estimate WOTS+ operation times based on hash speed."""
        # Your code here
        pass

testCases.input: hb = HashBenchmark(100); r = hb.benchmark_all(); 'sha256' in r

hints: Time many iterations, divide for average
l1 = ceil(8n / log2(w)), l2 for checksum

exercises.title: Cached Merkle Tree
exercises.description: Implement an efficiently cached Merkle tree.
exercises.task: Create a Merkle tree with strategic caching for repeated auth path queries.

testCases.input: ct = CachedMerkleTree(4); ct.initialize(b'seed'); ct.compute_auth_path(0); ct.compute_auth_path(1); ct.cache_stats()['hit_rate'] > 0

hints: Adjacent leaves share most of auth path

exercises.title: Parameter Selector
exercises.description: Implement a parameter selection tool for hash-based signatures.
exercises.task: Create a tool that recommends schemes based on requirements.
